comment:
/**
 * @summary The top-level HYPERBLAM element, setting the audio context for all element operations within it. 
 * @prop {number} [gain=1] - The output gain, sent to the destination. Think of it as the master volume.
 * @prop {number} [bpm=120] - The tempo, in beats per minute. Is overruled by a Sequencer element, if present.
 */
summary: The top-level HYPERBLAM element, setting the audio context for all element operations within it.

properties.description: The output gain, sent to the destination. Think of it as the master volume.
properties.name: gain

properties.description: The tempo, in beats per minute. Is overruled by a Sequencer element, if present.
properties.name: bpm

name: Audio

comment:
/**
 * @summary Organizes samples into a single bank for provisioning a sample-based instrument like Pads or Notes.
 * @event {CustomEvent} bank - Fired when all the Bank’s samples have been converted to buffers and are ready for use. The data contains the buffers as `sounds`.
 */
summary: Organizes samples into a single bank for provisioning a sample-based instrument like Pads or Notes.
name: The data contains the buffers as `sounds`.
memberof: {CustomEvent} bank - Fired when all the Bank’s samples have been converted to buffers and are ready for use
longname: {CustomEvent} bank - Fired when all the Bank’s samples have been converted to buffers and are ready for use.event: The data contains the buffers as `sounds`.

comment:
/**
 * @summary Defines a single bar’s pattern, as handled by a parent Track element.
 * @prop {string} s - The space-separated pattern representing the distribution of notes or sounds. `0` represents a pause (skipped beat). 
 * @prop {number} [chance=1] - The probability that the bar’s pattern (in its entirety) will be played.
 * @prop {boolean} [fill=false] - Whether to play a fill: a randomized rearrangement of the bar’s pattern.
 */
summary: Defines a single bar’s pattern, as handled by a parent Track element.

properties.description: The space-separated pattern representing the distribution of notes or sounds. `0` represents a pause (skipped beat).
properties.name: s

properties.description: The probability that the bar’s pattern (in its entirety) will be played.
properties.name: chance

properties.description: Whether to play a fill: a randomized rearrangement of the bar’s pattern.
properties.name: fill

name: Bar

summary: A multi-purpose event handling element, used for affecting props, `audioParams`, and triggering methods.
name: Blam

comment:
/**
 * @summary For connecting together different effects, like in a pedalboard.
 * @prop {string} [out=undefined] - The `id` of the next element to connect to, in the signal chain. If not set, it defaults to the ancestor Audio element.
 */
summary: For connecting together different effects, like in a pedalboard.

properties.description: The `id` of the next element to connect to, in the signal chain. If not set, it defaults to the ancestor Audio element.
properties.name: out

name: Chain

comment:
/**
 * @summary For tracking and showing any element’s prop’s value. 
 * @prop {string} [element=null] - A CSS selector representing the element to watch. Defaults to the `parentNode`
 * @prop {string} [prop=null] - The attribute defining the value to be displayed.
 */
summary: For tracking and showing any element’s prop’s value.

properties.description: A CSS selector representing the element to watch. Defaults to the `parentNode`
properties.name: element

properties.description: The attribute defining the value to be displayed.
properties.name: prop

name: Display

comment:
/**
 * @summary An echo effect, using a delay node. Includes a lowpass filter for tape echo-like degradation.
 * @prop {number} [beats=1] - The interval of echos, relative to the beats per minute set by an ancestor Audio or Sequencer element.
 * @prop {number} [feedback=0.5] - The intensity of the effect. When applying a `cutoff`, be aware that high values can cause self-oscillation (and things can get very loud).
 * @prop {number} [cutoff=null] - A lowpass filter, applied inside the feedback loop, meaning echos get progressively _duller_ over time. Emulates the behavior of tape echo.
 */
summary: An echo effect, using a delay node. Includes a lowpass filter for tape echo-like degradation.

properties.description: The interval of echos, relative to the beats per minute set by an ancestor Audio or Sequencer element.
properties.name: beats

properties.description: The intensity of the effect. When applying a `cutoff`, be aware that high values can cause self-oscillation (and things can get very loud).
properties.name: feedback

properties.description: A lowpass filter, applied inside the feedback loop, meaning echos get progressively _duller_ over time. Emulates the behavior of tape echo.
properties.name: cutoff

name: Echo

properties.description: Comma separated pairs of numbers representing the envelope. Each pair defines the value and the time.
properties.name: curve

/**
 * @summary A single-band filter effect. Important for coloring sound.
 * @prop {number} [freq=1000] - The frequency for the filter, in hertz. The role of this frequency depends on the `type`.
 * @prop {number} [q=1] - The quality factor (often relating to resonance).
 * @prop {string} [type=lowpass] - The type of filter. This can only be one of 'lowpass', 'highpass', 'bandpass', or 'notch'.
 */
summary: A single-band filter effect. Important for coloring sound.

properties.description: The frequency for the filter, in hertz. The role of this frequency depends on the `type`.
properties.name: freq

properties.name: type

/**
 * @summary A basic gain (volume) effect. Can be automated with an LFO to create tremolo effects or an Envelope to affect attack or delay.
 * @prop {number} [gain=1] - The gain of the gain node. This is relative to the input gain. The default value, `1`, means _no change_.
 */
summary: A basic gain (volume) effect. Can be automated with an LFO to create tremolo effects or an Envelope to affect attack or delay.

properties.description: The gain of the gain node. This is relative to the input gain. The default value, `1`, means _no change_.
properties.name: gain

name: Gain

comment:
/**
 * @summary A multi-purpose Low Frequency Oscillator. Modulate any `audioParam` of any element, including any other LFO. 
 * @prop {string} [wave=sine] - The waveform for the oscillator. Can be 'sine', 'square', 'sawtooth', or 'triangle'.
 * @prop {number} [gain=1] - For an LFO, the gain represents how much the `param` value changes in either direction. If the `param` value is `0.5` na the LFO `gain` value is `0.25`, the `param` value will oscillate between `0.25` and `0.75`.
 * @prop {number} [beats=1] - The frequency of the LFO in beats, mapped to the bpm of an ancestor Audio or Sequencer element.
 * @prop {boolean} [invert=false] - Reverses the waveform. `1` values become `-1` and vice versa.
 * @prop {string} param - The audioParam to which the LFO connects. Its value at the time of connection represents the _center_ value for oscillation.
 */
summary: A multi-purpose Low Frequency Oscillator. Modulate any `audioParam` of any element, including any other LFO.

properties.description: The waveform for the oscillator. Can be 'sine', 'square', 'sawtooth', or 'triangle'.
properties.name: wave

properties.description: For an LFO, the gain represents how much the `param` value changes in either direction. If the `param` value is `0.5` na the LFO `gain` value is `0.25`, the `param` value will oscillate between `0.25` and `0.75`.
properties.name: gain

properties.description: The frequency of the LFO in beats, mapped to the bpm of an ancestor Audio or Sequencer element.
properties.name: beats

properties.description: Reverses the waveform. `1` values become `-1` and vice versa.
properties.name: invert

properties.description: The audioParam to which the LFO connects. Its value at the time of connection represents the _center_ value for oscillation.
properties.name: param

name: LFO

/**
 * @summary Extracts discrete values from LFOs to be used as multipliers. Great for automating regular prop values over time.
 * @prop {string} [wave=triangle] - The waveform for the built-in LFO. Can be 'sine', 'square', 'sawtooth', or 'triangle'.
 * @prop {number} [gain=1] - Represents how much the multiplier value changes in either direction.
 * @prop {number} [beats=1] - The frequency of the built-in LFO in beats, mapped to the bpm of an ancestor Audio or Sequencer element.
 * @prop {boolean} [invert=false] - Reverses the LFO. `1` values become `-1` and vice versa.
 */

 * @summary A pitched instrument, using multiple samples to create tonal variety.
 * @augments Player
 * @prop {string} [root=C4] - The root note for the buffers/sounds where not supplied by the individual Sample. Used to calculate pitch shifts.
 * @prop {number} [notes=C4 D#4 G4] - A set of notes from which to make arpeggios. By default, it’s a basic C Minor triad.
 */
summary: A pitched instrument, using multiple samples to create tonal variety.

comment:
/**
 * @summary Handles `midiOn` events, typically for playing an instrument’s sounds.
 * @prop {string} [device=null] - The named MIDI device to listen to. If not set, the element listens to all connected devices.
 * @prop {string} [root=C4] - The key on the MIDI keyboard from which all sounds will be mapped.
 * @prop {number} [range=null] - How many keys, from the `root`, will be mapped (will actively make sounds). If `null`, it’s the whole keyboard.
 * @prop {boolean} [spread=false] - For non-tonal instruments like Pads. Whether to spread the individual sounds over the range of keys (one sound per key).
 */
summary: Handles `midiOn` events, typically for playing an instrument’s sounds.

properties.description: The named MIDI device to listen to. If not set, the element listens to all connected devices.
properties.name: device

properties.description: The key on the MIDI keyboard from which all sounds will be mapped.
properties.name: root

properties.description: How many keys, from the `root`, will be mapped (will actively make sounds). If `null`, it’s the whole keyboard.
properties.name: range

properties.description: For non-tonal instruments like Pads. Whether to spread the individual sounds over the range of keys (one sound per key).
properties.name: spread

/**
 * @summary A classic phaser effect. The sound of a signal going in and out of phase with itself.
 * @prop {number} [beats=1] - The length of the phasing effect, relative to the bpm.
 * @prop {number} [center=4000] - The frequency phasing centers around.
 * @prop {number} [depth=4000] - The distance from the `center` frequency in both directions.
 * @prop {number} [q=1] - The quality factor. The resonance of the effect.
 */
summary: A classic phaser effect. The sound of a signal going in and out of phase with itself.

properties.description: The length of the phasing effect, relative to the bpm.
properties.name: beats

properties.description: The frequency phasing centers around.
properties.name: center

properties.description: The distance from the `center` frequency in both directions.
properties.name: depth

properties.description: The quality factor. The resonance of the effect.
properties.name: q

/**
 * @summary Convolution based reverb effect using a sample from a Sample element as the impulse response (IR).
 * @prop {string} [src=null] - The Sample element supplying the impulse response for convolution. Supply an `id` with this prop, or just supply a child Sample element.
 * @prop {number} [cutoff=10000] - The cutoff frequency for the reverb. The lower the value, the _darker_ the reverb.
 */

properties.description: The Sample element supplying the impulse response for convolution. Supply an `id` with this prop, or just supply a child Sample element.

comment:
/**
 * @summary Converts a sound file into a buffer that can be triggered as a sound by an instrument.
 * @prop {number} [src=null] - The path to the sample sound file. Typically, this would be an mp3.
 * @prop {string} [root=C4] - The note the sample represents, if it is tonal. Used for calculating pitch changes.
 * @event {CustomEvent} sample - Fired when the buffer has been created. The buffer itself is passed in the event data.
 */
summary: Converts a sound file into a buffer that can be triggered as a sound by an instrument.

properties.description: The path to the sample sound file. Typically, this would be an mp3.
properties.name: src

properties.description: The note the sample represents, if it is tonal. Used for calculating pitch changes.
properties.name: root

name: The buffer itself is passed in the event data.
memberof: {CustomEvent} sample - Fired when the buffer has been created
longname: {CustomEvent} sample - Fired when the buffer has been created.event: The buffer itself is passed in the event data.

comment:
/**
 * @summary Schedules future beats in a sequence and alerts subscribing elements.
 * @prop {number} [bpm=120] - The tempo in beats per minute. Overrules the Audio element’s bpm.
 * @prop {boolean|number} [swing=null] - Applied as a Boolean, it applies a swing of `0.333`, making adjacent beats the first and last beats of a triplet. Supply a number to adjust this value.
 * @event {CustomEvent} blam - Critically contains the time for the scheduled beat, but also supplies Sequencer and Track information.
 */
summary: Schedules future beats in a sequence and alerts subscribing elements.

properties.description: The tempo in beats per minute. Overrules the Audio element’s bpm.
properties.name: bpm

properties.description: Applied as a Boolean, it applies a swing of `0.333`, making adjacent beats the first and last beats of a triplet. Supply a number to adjust this value.
properties.name: swing

name: but also supplies Sequencer and Track information.
memberof: {CustomEvent} blam - Critically contains the time for the scheduled beat
longname: {CustomEvent} blam - Critically contains the time for the scheduled beat,event: but also supplies Sequencer and Track information.

summary: Takes an input of type `checkbox` and uses it to change any target prop of any element.
name: Switch
Switch
summary: Takes an input of type `checkbox` and uses it to change any target prop of any element.
Takes a child button element and uses it to trigger any method of any element.
name: Tap
Tap
summary: Takes a child button element and uses it to trigger any method of any element.

comment:
/**
 * @summary Corresponds to a Sequencer and uses child Bar elements to define repeating rhythmic patterns.
 * @prop {boolean} [solo=false] - When applied, all other Track elements belonging to the ancestor Sequencer are suspended (their sounds aren’t played).
 * @prop {string} [override=null] - A space-separated list of Track `id`s. Tracks whose sounds will _not_ play when this Track plays a sound. 
 * @event {CustomEvent} blam - Fired whenever one bar (defined by a child Bar element) ends and another begins.
 */
summary: Corresponds to a Sequencer and uses child Bar elements to define repeating rhythmic patterns.

properties.description: When applied, all other Track elements belonging to the ancestor Sequencer are suspended (their sounds aren’t played).
properties.name: solo

properties.description: A space-separated list of Track `id`s. Tracks whose sounds will _not_ play when this Track plays a sound.
properties.name: override

name: {CustomEvent} blam - Fired whenever one bar (defined by a child Bar element) ends and another begins.
longname: event:{CustomEvent} blam - Fired whenever one bar (defined by a child Bar element) ends and another begins.
